source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - WD-152 A Platform Crane Failure

context.region: Gulf of America, West Delta Block 152

actions_taken: Crane boom secured with ratchets and straps to prevent further falling
Crane boom removed completely and transported to third-party crane service facility
Boom cables, load block, pendant lines, hoses, fittings and bridle assembly sent for investigation
Wire rope samples sent to third-party laboratory for testing and analysis
BSEE investigation conducted with on-site visits on 05 September, 11 September, and 12 September 2025
event.summary: On 27 August 2025 at 1045 hours, the WD-152 A platform crane failed during a dynamic lift of an 8,000-pound Power Pack from a Motor Vessel. The crane's boom cable separated, causing the crane boom to fall uncontrollably and strike platform handrails and the vessel below. The root cause was a failed bearing in the boom cable bridle sheave assembly that caused excessive wear to the sheave pin, allowing the cable to cut through the sheave and ultimately fail under load. No injuries occurred, but damage was sustained to platform handrails, the crane assembly, and the Motor Vessel. A small amount of hydraulic fluid was released into the Gulf of America.
recommendations: BSEE New Orleans District recommends the Office of Incident Investigations consider issuing a Safety Alert regarding the incident
Renaissance to update crane safe work practices to require annual removal of sheaves and sheave pins for inspection
Third-party crane service to re-write procedures to remove pins for inspections per customer requests

hazards.name: Mechanical failure of crane lifting equipment

threats.name: Failed bearing in bridle sheave assembly
Excessive wear on sheave pin due to bearing failure
Boom cable cutting through sheave and failing under load
Loss of boom cable tension and support

consequences.name: Impact damage to Motor Vessel stern and jump deck
Release of hydraulic fluid to Gulf of America
Potential personnel injury from falling equipment

supporting_text: The last crane inspection, other than a crane pre-use, that was performed on the crane prior to the incident was an annual inspection on 01 August 2025. During this inspection, the Crane Inspector checked off in his paperwork that the bridle sheaves and boom cable were in good condition at the time of the inspection.

The Crane Inspector stated that during the inspection of the bridle sheaves, he lowered the bridle to the crane boom and provided slack in the boom cable to allow him to move the sheaves freely. He then rolled/turned the sheaves to ensure they moved freely without cable tension. He stated that he greased the sheaves by using the greasing points on the sheave pin. The Inspector also stated that he did not remove the sheave pin to inspect it.

controls.name: Sheave pin removal and inspection
controls.barrier_role: Detection of bearing wear and pin degradation

supporting_text: Investigation findings revealed the lack of inspection of the sheave pins during crane inspections. Renaissance stated that they failed to include scheduled inspection intervals of specific sheaves, pins, and bearings in their crane inspection policy.
The crane inspector stated he was not required to remove the sheave pin per company policies and the API RP 2D Sixth edition that they follow.
The third-party crane service provided their sheave inspection training guide, and the training did not specify the removal of the sheave pin during sheave inspections.

controls.name: Crane load chart compliance and boom angle monitoring
controls.barrier_role: Ensure loads remain within safe working limits

supporting_text: The crane operator was operating the crane at 61–65-degree boom angles when the incident occurred. The crane's load chart states that at a 61-degree boom angle, the crane's dynamic Safe Working Load (SWL) is 12,200 pounds, placing the load being lifted well within the crane's capacity at the angle during the time of the incident.

controls.name: Crane pre-use inspection and pull test
controls.barrier_role: Verify crane operational readiness before use

supporting_text: The Crane Inspector also performed a pull test on the crane on 02 August 2025 which successfully pulled 30,900 pounds at a 74-degree boom angle and a 25-foot radius according to the inspector's paperwork.

controls.name: Communication between crane operator and vessel captain
controls.barrier_role: Coordinate safe positioning for lifting operations

supporting_text: After notifying the MV Captain of the next lift, the MV Captain repositioned the MV so the Crane Operator could safely make the lift. The MV then moved too close to the platform for the Crane Operator to comfortably make the lift, so the Crane Operator asked the MV captain to move forward (away from the platform).

controls.name: Post-incident boom securing and removal
controls.barrier_role: Prevent secondary falling of damaged boom and components

supporting_text: After the incident occurred and all personnel were safe, Renaissance decided to secure the crane boom that was hanging off the side of the platform with ratchets and straps. This was performed to reduce the possibility of the crane boom or other components falling into the Gulf of America. Later, Renaissance removed the boom completely to prevent any possibilities of the crane boom falling.

controls.name: Wire rope condition and fatigue analysis
controls.barrier_role: Identify cable degradation and failure modes

supporting_text: Renaissance elected to send the sections of the boom cable that were broken during the incident to have the cable tested and analyzed at a third-party laboratory. The analysis report drafted by third-party laboratory concluded that the wire rope showed no signs of corrosion near the failed cable areas or any physical damage such as kinks, abrasions, heat or flat spots. The analysis did however reveal features of ductile failure that included micro void coalescence, shear dimples, and localized plastic deformation with necking.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
